cards.title: Longevity Science — Hallmarks of Ageing
cards._legacyHtml:
The 9 Hallmarks of Ageing (López-Otín 2013, updated 2023)
Understanding the biological mechanisms of ageing enables targeted naturopathic and lifestyle interventions to slow the ageing process, extend healthspan, and reduce age-related disease burden. Naturopathic practice is uniquely positioned for longevity medicine — diet, movement, stress, sleep, and fasting target multiple hallmarks simultaneously.
HallmarkMechanismKey Naturopathic InterventionBiomarker
Genomic InstabilityDNA damage accumulation — oxidative, radiation, replication errorsAntioxidants (NAC, astaxanthin, vitamin C), reducing mutagenic exposures, cruciferous vegetables (NRF2 activation)8-OHdG (oxidative DNA damage in urine)
Telomere AttritionTelomere shortening → cell senescence → inflammationMeditation ★★ (Blackburn Nobel prize — telomerase activation), exercise ★★★, omega-3 ★★, astragalus (TA-65 — commercial telomerase activator)Telomere length (leukocyte) — specialist lab
Epigenetic AlterationsMethylation drift, histone changes → gene expression dysregulationMethyl donors (folate, B12, SAMe, TMG), caloric restriction, fasting, exercise — all remodel epigenome. Horvath clock measures epigenetic ageDNA methylation age (Horvath clock) — specialist
Loss of ProteostasisMisfolded protein accumulation (amyloid, tau) — autophagy declineIntermittent fasting / prolonged fasting → autophagy activation ★★★. Spermidine (wheat germ, aged cheese). Resveratrol (SIRT1 activation)Oxidised proteins, heat shock proteins
Deregulated Nutrient SensingmTOR hyperactivation, ↓ AMPK, ↓ sirtuins, ↓ IGF-1 pathway dysregulationCaloric restriction, fasting (↓ mTOR, ↑ AMPK), berberine (AMPK activator), exercise ★★★, metformin (conventional)IGF-1, insulin, mTOR activity markers
Mitochondrial Dysfunction↓ ATP, ↑ ROS, mitophagy failureCoQ10 200–600mg/day, NAD+ precursors (NMN/NR), PQQ 20mg/day, exercise (mitochondrial biogenesis via PGC-1α), cold exposureLactate:pyruvate ratio, CoQ10 plasma, mtDNA copy number
Cellular SenescenceSASP (senescence-associated secretory phenotype) → chronic inflammation (inflammaging)Quercetin 1g/day + dasatinib (senolytic — conventional). Fisetin (strawberries) ★. Fasting cycles. Curcumin (anti-SASP)p16, p21, IL-6, IL-8 (SASP markers)
Stem Cell Exhaustion↓ Regenerative capacity → tissue repair failureExercise ★★★ (mobilises bone marrow stem cells), fasting (stem cell rejuvenation — Longo research), Vit D, omega-3, reduce xenobioticsCD34+ cells, telomere length in stem cells
Altered Intercellular Communication↑ Inflammatory cytokines (inflammaging), ↓ hormones, gut dysbiosisAnti-inflammatory diet ★★★, intermittent fasting, stress reduction (cortisol normalisation), optimise microbiomeCRP, IL-6, TNF-α, GDF-11, klotho

cards.title: Longevity — Evidence-Based Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
1
Exercise — The Most Powerful Longevity Intervention Zone 2 aerobic training (conversational pace — 60–70% HRmax) 150–180 min/week: mitochondrial biogenesis, insulin sensitivity, autophagy, brain BDNF. VO₂max is the single strongest predictor of longevity — stronger than smoking, BP, or cholesterol. Strength training 2–3×/week: sarcopenia prevention, bone density, metabolic health, IGF-1 stimulation. HIIT 1–2×/week: mitochondrial uncoupling proteins, maximum cardiovascular adaptation.
2
Longevity Nutrition — Blue Zones Principles Plant-predominant (95% plants, 5% animal): legumes daily (lentils, chickpeas — fibre → butyrate → anti-inflammatory). Caloric restriction 15–25% (best longevity evidence in all species studied). Time-restricted eating 8–10hr window (activates autophagy, improves circadian biology). Anti-inflammatory spices daily: turmeric (curcumin), ginger, cinnamon. Moderate protein (1g/kg/day — mTOR: high protein accelerates ageing in middle age but may benefit after 65). Minimise ultra-processed food, refined sugars, trans fats.
3
Fasting Protocols for Longevity Intermittent fasting 16:8 daily: ↑ AMPK, ↓ mTOR, ↑ autophagy, improved insulin sensitivity. Prolonged fasting 3–5 days 2–4×/year (Longo protocol — Fasting Mimicking Diet): IGF-1 ↓ 60%, stem cell regeneration, immune system rejuvenation. Ramadan fasting research: significant improvements in cardiometabolic markers, gut microbiome diversity. Time fasting: last meal by 6–7pm (circadian advantage — evening eating suppresses autophagy and disrupts clock genes).
4
Sleep — The Non-Negotiable Longevity Pillar 7–9 hours quality sleep is non-negotiable for longevity. During sleep: glymphatic system clears amyloid-beta (Alzheimer's prevention). Growth hormone peaks in deep sleep (tissue repair, fat metabolism). Testosterone synthesis is sleep-dependent. <6hrs sleep = 4× increased all-cause mortality, 50% ↑ cardiovascular events, accelerated Alzheimer's risk. Naturopathic sleep optimisation: magnesium glycinate 400mg, melatonin 0.5–1mg (low dose more effective than 5mg), sleep hygiene, yoga Nidra, CBT-I.
5
Stress & Longevity — Hormesis Principle Chronic stress accelerates ageing (telomere shortening, cortisol-driven inflammation, neurodegeneration). BUT acute, controlled stress (hormesis) promotes longevity: cold exposure, HIIT, fasting, sauna — all are hermetic stressors that activate NRF2, SIRT1, AMPK, heat shock proteins. Mindfulness meditation: reduces IL-6, increases telomerase activity, reduces epigenetic age. Sense of purpose: 7-year longer life expectancy (Ikigai — Okinawa longevity factor).

cards.title: Longevity Supplements — Evidence Review
cards._legacyHtml:
SupplementDoseMechanismEvidenceCautions
NAD+ Precursors (NMN/NR)NMN 500mg/day or NR 300–500mg/dayNAD+ declines 50% by age 60 → ↓ SIRT1/3, ↓ PARP (DNA repair), ↓ energy metabolism. Supplementation restores NAD+★★ Human RCTs show NAD+ restoration — functional outcomes still emergingExpensive. Some concern NMN may feed cancer cells in susceptible individuals — active cancer: caution
Resveratrol250–500mg/day with fatSIRT1 activator, anti-inflammatory, telomerase activation, epigenetic remodelling★★ Strong animal data. Human trials mixed — bioavailability poor (pterostilbene more bioavailable)High dose may inhibit CYP3A4 — drug interactions. Oestrogen-sensitive cancers: caution (weak phytooestrogen)
Quercetin500–1000mg/daySenolytic (clears senescent cells), AMPK activator, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant★★ Animal longevity data strong. Human RCTs: reduced senescent cell burden (combined with dasatinib)CYP3A4 inhibitor at high doses. Iron absorption inhibition — separate from iron supplements
Spermidine1–2mg/day (dietary) or supplementPotent autophagy inducer (polyamine pathway). Cardioprotective, neuroprotective★★★ Population study: higher dietary spermidine = 5-year longer lifespan (n=829). RCT: improved memory in older adultsWell tolerated. Found in wheat germ, aged cheese, mushrooms, soy. Supplement if dietary insufficient
Fisetin100mg/day or 20mg/kg pulsed 2 days/monthStrongest senolytic flavonoid (strawberries, apples). Crosses BBB — neuroprotective★★ Animal data very strong (30% lifespan extension in mice). Human RCTs underway (Mayo Clinic)Generally well tolerated. Pulsed high-dose protocol based on animal senolytic research — dose uncertain in humans
Magnesium400–500mg/day glycinate/threonateCofactor for 300+ enzymes, DNA repair (Mg-ATP), telomere maintenance, glucose metabolism★★★ Deficiency associated with accelerated ageing, ↑ inflammation, cardiovascular disease, diabetesRenal failure — use with caution. Glycinate best tolerated. Threonate crosses BBB — best for cognitive longevity
🟡 Longevity Biomarker Panel
Recommended longevity monitoring panel: HbA1c (metabolic ageing), fasting insulin + HOMA-IR (insulin sensitivity), hs-CRP (inflammaging), homocysteine (methylation), 25(OH)Vit D, omega-3 index (EPA+DHA % in RBC — target >8%), testosterone + DHEAS (hormonal ageing), IGF-1, CoQ10 plasma, GGT (liver stress / oxidative load), Lp(a) (cardiovascular longevity risk), APOB (atherogenic particle number), VO₂max (functional fitness — single best longevity predictor). Review every 6–12 months and titrate interventions to biomarkers.

cards.title: Anti-Ageing Skin & Aesthetic Naturopathy
cards._legacyHtml:
InterventionMechanismProtocolEvidence
Collagen peptidesStimulates fibroblast collagen synthesis. Provides hydroxyproline-proline dipeptides as substrate10g/day hydrolysed collagen + Vit C 500mg (co-factor for collagen cross-linking)★★★ RCT: significant skin elasticity improvement after 8 weeks
Vitamin C (topical + oral)Antioxidant — neutralises UV-induced ROS. Cofactor for collagen synthesis. Inhibits melanogenesisOral: 1–2g/day. Topical: L-ascorbic acid 10–20% serum (AM)★★★ Well established for photoprotection and collagen synthesis
AstaxanthinMost potent carotenoid antioxidant (6000× stronger than Vit C for singlet oxygen). Anti-photoageing6–12mg/day oral + topical cream★★★ RCT: reduced wrinkle depth, improved skin elasticity, reduced UV damage
Silica (horsetail extract)Structural component of connective tissue. Stimulates collagen and keratin synthesisHorsetail extract 300–500mg/day (standardised to silica). Biotin 5mg/day for hair/nail synergy★★ Improved skin hydration, nail strength, hair tensile strength
Intermittent FastingAutophagy removes damaged cellular proteins and organelles — including in skin cells. ↓ AGEs (advanced glycation end-products — primary skin ageing driver)16:8 daily. Reduce dietary sugar (AGE formation). Avoid smoking (↑ AGE production 10×)★★★ AGE reduction correlates directly with skin ageing deceleration
